title: 802.1X Authentication Failure Analysis
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, RADIUS TA
spl:
index=nac (sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" OR sourcetype="radius:auth") earliest=-7d
| search "802.1X" OR auth_method="EAP*" OR eap_method=*
| where match(lower(message),"(?i)fail|reject|denied")
| stats count by eap_method, failure_reason, nas_ip
| sort 30 -count
description: Breaks down 802.1X/EAP failures by method, failure reason, and NAS to pinpoint certificate rollout issues vs brute-force vs misconfigured supplicants.
value: Network security teams categorize 802.1X authentication failures by ISE-specific failure reason, separating systemic issues (certificate/CA/AD problems) from individual user problems (expired passwords, wrong config), enabling targeted remediation instead of blanket troubleshooting.
implementation: Extract `eap_method`, `failure_reason`, and `nas_ip` per your TA. Alert on spikes in a single failure bucket (e.g., TLS cert errors). Compare before/after cert updates.
visualization: Bar chart (failures by EAP method), Table (top NAS + reason), Line chart (daily failure rate).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Authentication pattern spikes during ISE or RADIUS maintenance, certificate renewal windows, or RADIUS time-outs that affect many clients at once.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco ISE RADIUS logs in `index=nac` with `sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`. Key ISE message IDs: 5400 (authentication failed), 5405 (RADIUS request dropped), 5440 (endpoint abandoned EAP). Key fields: `FailureReason` (ISE-specific reason string), `UserName`, `CallingStationID`, `NetworkDeviceName`, `NAS-Port-Id`, `EapAuthentication` (EAP-TLS/PEAP/EAP-FAST), `DetailedInfo`.
• ISE FailureReason codes are specific and diagnostic: '22056: Subject not found in the applicable identity store' means the certificate CN is not in AD. '12514: EAP-TLS failed SSL/TLS handshake because of an unknown CA in the client certificates chain' means the endpoint certificate was issued by an untrusted CA.
• Create a `failure_reason_map.csv` lookup: `failure_reason,category,severity,typical_fix` mapping ISE failure reasons to operational categories.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify failure data:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-24h
| where match(AuthenticationStatus, "(?i)fail")
| stats count by FailureReason
| sort - count
```
This shows all unique failure reasons and their frequency. The top 3 usually account for 80% of failures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Failure categorization:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-24h
| where match(AuthenticationStatus, "(?i)fail")
| lookup failure_reason_map.csv failure_reason as FailureReason OUTPUT category severity typical_fix
| stats count dc(CallingStationID) as devices dc(UserName) as users by category, severity, FailureReason, typical_fix
| sort severity, - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Maps ISE failure reasons to operational categories (certificate, credential, policy, infrastructure) and severity levels. A single failure reason affecting many users is a systemic issue (policy misconfiguration, certificate expiry, AD connectivity). A single failure reason affecting one user is a user-specific problem (wrong password, expired account, revoked certificate).

EAP method analysis:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-7d
| where match(AuthenticationStatus, "(?i)fail")
| stats count dc(CallingStationID) as devices by EapAuthentication, FailureReason
| sort EapAuthentication, - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Compare top failure reasons in Splunk with ISE Operations > RADIUS > Live Logs > Failed filter.
(b) Generate a known failure (wrong password) and verify the correct FailureReason appears.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Failure count by category (pie). Row 2 — Top failure reasons table. Row 3 — EAP method failure comparison.
Alert: Trigger when a single FailureReason exceeds 100 affected devices in 1 hour.

Runbook:
1. **Certificate failures spike** — Check RADIUS server certificate expiry. Check CA trust chain.
2. **'Subject not found' spike** — AD sync issue. Check ISE-AD join status.
3. **Single user repeated failures** — Account locked, password expired, or wrong supplicant config.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **FailureReason is generic** — Enable 'Failed Attempts' logging in ISE with detailed attribute logging.
• **No EAP method field** — Requires parsing from the ISE detailed syslog format, not the standard format.
grandmaExplanation: When devices fail to authenticate to our network, the system gives a specific reason — like the certificate is wrong or the account is locked. We group these reasons into categories so we can quickly tell whether it is one person's problem or a big issue affecting everyone.
